root_text.an5.27:1.2: Samādhiṁ, bhikkhave, bhāvayataṁ appamāṇaṁ nipakānaṁ patissatānaṁ pañca ñāṇāni paccattaññeva uppajjanti.
root_text.an5.27:1.4: ‘Ayaṁ samādhi paccuppannasukho ceva āyatiñca sukhavipāko’ti paccattaññeva ñāṇaṁ uppajjati,
root_text.an5.27:1.5: ‘ayaṁ samādhi ariyo nirāmiso’ti paccattaññeva ñāṇaṁ uppajjati,
root_text.an5.27:1.7: ‘ayaṁ samādhi santo paṇīto paṭippassaddhaladdho ekodibhāvādhigato, na sasaṅkhāraniggayhavāritagato’ti paccattaññeva ñāṇaṁ uppajjati,
root_text.an5.27:1.8: ‘sato kho panāhaṁ imaṁ samāpajjāmi sato vuṭṭhahāmī’ti paccattaññeva ñāṇaṁ uppajjati.
root_text.an5.27:2.1: Samādhiṁ, bhikkhave, bhāvetha appamāṇaṁ nipakā patissatā.
root_text.an5.27:2.2: Samādhiṁ, bhikkhave, bhāvayataṁ appamāṇaṁ nipakānaṁ patissatānaṁ imāni pañca ñāṇāni paccattaññeva uppajjantī”ti.

translation_text.an5.27:1.1: “Mendicants, develop limitless immersion, alert and mindful.
translation_text.an5.27:1.2: When you develop limitless immersion, alert and mindful, five knowledges arise for you personally.
translation_text.an5.27:1.4: ‘This immersion is blissful now, and results in bliss in the future.’ …
translation_text.an5.27:1.5: ‘This immersion is noble and not of the flesh.’ …
translation_text.an5.27:1.6: ‘This immersion is not cultivated by reprobates.’ …
translation_text.an5.27:1.7: ‘This immersion is peaceful and sublime and tranquil and unified, not held in place by forceful suppression.’ …
translation_text.an5.27:1.8: ‘I mindfully enter into and emerge from this immersion.’ …
translation_text.an5.27:2.1: Develop limitless immersion, alert and mindful.
translation_text.an5.27:2.2: When you develop limitless immersion, alert and mindful, these five knowledges arise for you personally.”

variant_text.an5.27:1.7: na sasaṅkhāraniggayhavāritagato’ti → na ca sasaṅkhāraniggayhavāritacatoti (bj); na ca sasaṅkhāraniggayhavāritappattoti (sya-all); na ca sasaṅkhāraniggayhavāritāvaṭoti (pts1ed); na ca sasaṅkhāraniggayhavārivāvaṭoti (mr); na sasaṅkhāraniggayhavāriyādhigatoti (?)
variant_text.an5.27:1.8: sato vuṭṭhahāmī’ti → sato ca uṭṭhahāmīti (bj); sato vuṭṭhahāmīti (sya-all, km); so kho panāhaṁ imaṁ samādhiṁ satova samāpajjāmi satova vuṭṭhahāmīti (pts1ed)
